steps.title: Set the equations equal to solve for x
steps.content:
Since both equations are set equal to $y$, we can equate them:
$$-\frac{1}{5}x = \frac{1}{7}x$$

steps.title: Rearrange and factor to find x
steps.content:
Move all terms to one side:
$$-\frac{1}{5}x - \frac{1}{7}x = 0$$

Factor out $x$:
$$x\left(-\frac{1}{5} - \frac{1}{7}\right) = 0$$

For a product to equal zero, one of the factors must be zero. The coefficient $-\frac{1}{5} - \frac{1}{7} = -\frac{12}{35}$ is non-zero, so $x = 0$. That matches choice B — B is correct.
